title: senna pendula var. eriocarpa
intro: Senna pendula var. eriocarpa, also known as the desert senna or snowball senna, is a plant species in the legume family Fabaceae. It is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, known for its medicinal properties derived from its seeds. The seeds of Senna pendula var. eriocarpa contain anthraquinone glycosides, which have been traditionally used to stimulate bowel movements, treat constipation, and aid in weight loss. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tea or capsules, can be used to support digestive health and promote regularity, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
